questions.question: Name something you should do when a child is crying at drop-off.

answers.text: Get down to eye level
Use a calm, warm voice
Offer a simple distraction or job
Reassure the parent briefly and confidently
Smile and stay relaxed
Give the child a small job to feel important

questions.question: Name a way to get a group's attention without yelling.

answers.text: Stop and wait instead of competing
Use a call-and-response or signal
Move closer to the group
Keep directions short and clear
Lower your voice so they lean in
Use a countdown (3, 2, 1, eyes on me)

questions.question: Name a way to include a kid who's standing alone.

answers.text: Go to them quickly
Use their name
Pair them with a kind peer
Invite them into a specific role or task
Get down to their level and start a conversation
Bring the group's activity to them

questions.question: Name something that is NOT allowed under the dress code.

answers.text: Sports bras, crop tops, or halter tops
Shorts shorter than mid-thigh
Leggings or yoga pants
Transparent clothing (incl. when wet)
Unsafe footwear during activities
Shirts that extend past the shorts

questions.question: Name a rule from the Restroom Rule of 2.

answers.text: At least 2 campers and 2 volunteers present
No camper alone in the restroom
Volunteers do not enter stalls
Two volunteers present if assistance is needed
Captain must be informed before going
No restroom by the Story Café

questions.question: Name a rule about cell phones at camp.

answers.text: No phone use during camp hours (8:15am–12:45pm)
Keep phones put away while campers are on campus
Keep phones silenced
Repeated violations = phone goes to the Phone Hotel
Verbal warning given first
Continued violations = parent/guardian notified

questions.question: Name a rule about camper pickup.

answers.text: Only release to adults with pickup wristband
Never release a camper if you're unsure
Know who is NOT permitted to pick up a camper
Follow any parent-noted restrictions carefully
Contact your Color Captain if uncertain
Stay with the camper until pickup is complete

questions.question: Name something a volunteer must NEVER do with a camper.

answers.text: Be alone one-on-one
Use physical discipline
Text or DM them outside of church
Touch them in a way that makes them uncomfortable
Take or post photos of them
Release them without a pickup wristband

questions.question: Name something that makes a great KidsGames volunteer.

answers.text: Leads by example in attitude, words, and actions
Jumps in to help wherever needed
Shows up on time
Stays engaged the whole morning
Encourages campers with kindness and patience
Encourages other volunteers too

questions.question: A camper runs up and gives you a big hug. Name an appropriate response.

answers.text: A side hug — YES!
A high five
A front hug — NOT okay
A fist bump
Verbal encouragement (So good to see you!)
Gently redirect without making them feel rejected

questions.question: Name a red flag that should make you pause and check in with staff.

answers.text: A volunteer alone one-on-one with a camper
Any inappropriate touch
A camper who says they were hurt or feel unsafe
A private conversation away from others
A volunteer contacting a camper outside of church
A volunteer ignoring safety rules

questions.question: Name something every volunteer should know about Safety First.

answers.text: Bathroom Rule of 2: 2 volunteers, 2 campers
Never be alone with a camper
Always stay in sight of others
Side hugs or high fives only — no front hugs or piggybacks
Red wristbands = allergies — stay alert
Always know where your campers are
See something? Say something!
